item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My favorite bit of this is

this could be due to the ambient effects of the subway.

for the casual suggestion that New York's subways are just inherently nexii of eldritch forces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Totally agree: this SCiP has everything to be on the J list, but is curiously on the main list. The only explication I can understand is the fear of a lack of visibility, but it isn't a reason for posting it there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a downvote for me.

Here's my two cents
Close

I don't mind a silly concept, but if you do it in a silly way, then that just end up silly in the bad way.
I disagree with most comments which said it's a good build-up/punchline construct. It seems to me that as soon as you know the premice, you know that they'll use it to avoid subway problem for their own good.

There's also several illogical things : like, it shouldn't be Thaumiel. It doesn't help containment or the Foundation. It just help one O5 who used it for its own profit. Secondly, it seems very unlikely to me that one of the leader of the most powerful organisation in the world would use the subway anyway. And finally, I hardly see how the Foundation would have found out about this anomaly.

I don't say you can't do anything with it either. It could work as a J with some modification, it could even work as regular article if you dropped the silly payoff at the end, and explored more the concept. But for the moment it's just a joke about new york subway to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very good article.
The scp was original and fun to read.
The article was designed greatly and it was humouristic.
You get an upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In 2018, it was found that an O5 Directive had led to SCP-5983 being tested 297 times since its discovery 
 In my defense, if you've ever been on the 8:30 to Broadway, you'd want to nuke something too.

I can't stop laughing after thinking who that O5 make such an Directive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just want to know how do people come up with these ideas.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It probably should have been previously Thaumiel, but now (by way of O5 vote) reclassified.

Regardless, this is a great "setup, punchline" style article. The twist was pretty easy to see coming, but the article's short enough that it isn't to its detriment.

item.title: Re: Why Thamiel?
item.content:encoded: Probably because the O5 council was using it.

item.title: Why Thamiel?
item.content:encoded: Great idea, but why's it a Thamiel?

item.title: SCP-J?
item.content:encoded:
This is a wonderful read.

I love the visual flair given to this SCP. The punchline is humorous and the set up was phenomenal. It feels very polished all in all.

Echoing the other comments though, I agree that this doesn't have enough substance to not be a -J. The description is particularly short and no exploration of the areas and connect was made. I can't help but feel that the article exists only for the joke. If this is to stay in this series, I would love an attempt at exploring the areas and relationship. Why that particular location? What happens if the 05 push the limits? Is there side effects? If this gets a rewrite, I'd love to reread.

For now: no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say that having obscure vocabulary works in favor of the technical feel. In short, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe it was designated as Thaumiel because the 05 Council was utilizing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So you really don't consider it to be a -J?
Really laugh out loudly +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This fucking owns.

I would be unsurprised to find this as "PROBABILITY: ZERO" in Analog SF Magazine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An uniquely executed SCP. This page was funny, short and all round nonsensical.

In short, you get a big old plus one!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kant counters failed to return an accurate measure of the area's Hume level, though this could be due to the ambient effects of the subway.

Scrolled up to upvote at this line because it made me laugh and is 100% accurate to reality

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. The conclusion actually got a laugh out of me. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like funny skips. But this one is just a punchline. No-vote because I don't mind the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I came, I read, I laughed, and the little punchline at the end with everyone but 12 voting "yes" tickled me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A little bit too on the nose for me to upvote. Sort of leaves the same bad taste in my mouth SCP-3128 does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novoting. It shouldn't be Thaumiel and the whole thing is a bit too random. Also, not everyone knows what MTA stands for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's funny but:

- Why Thamiel? I'd rate it as Safe
- Given it's short length and humor, I was expecting this to be a -J article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A grand article with a nonsensical premise that feels definitively anomalous (plus a funny punchline)

+1

edit: I'm unsure why it's rated as Thaumiel if it's not really used to contain anything, would probably be better as Euclid or Safe. doesn't take away from my rating though

I'm gonna disagree with Roget here. This starts with a silly premise (and I thought we were going to do a correlation vs. causation thing) and stays on that note basically the whole way through. When we get to "we were seeing testing happen multiple times a day" I correctly guessed where it was heading.

This is a great example of buildup to payoff ratio done well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really random and the punchline, while funny, isn’t funny enough for me to get over the randomness. You could probably take that same punchline, write a totally different article about it and it would make just as much sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-5983 is an anomalous relationship between the force of an explosion in Drawing County, Wyoming, and the efficiency of the New York City Subway. "

Well…

this is surely something.

+1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so fucking funny

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's very satisfyingly written and I love the idea. It gives me an itch that this doesn't scratch in the end though. No voting for now.
Fuck Manhattan bound subways

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ounderhouse

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very unique concept with a relatively satisfying execution.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
JAMCON: DAY 1

explosions, i guess

thanks to magnadeus, kirby, nico, and a few people off discord for their eyes on this before posting. not my first idea for this theme, but my first draft for it. hope you enjoy.

image
– hide block

ROUNDERPAGE.
